name: Stir-Fried Cucumber with Pork
recipeInstructions:
1. Don't add other vegetables to this dish. Keep it simple. Just be artful in cutting the vegetables.
2. Preparation: Slice pork butt across grain, then into strips 1/4 inch thick by 2 inches long. Marinate with soy sauce, sherry & sugar for 15 minutes.
3. Peel cucumber; slice in half the long way and remove seeds; slice in strips to match pork; marinate in salt water for 15 minutes.
4. Stir-frying: Heat wok until very hot; add 1/2 of oil. Drain pork; reserve liquid. When oil just begins to smoke, add pork and stir-fry rapidly for about 1 minute until shrivelled. Remove pork from wok; wash wok. Reheat wok until very hot; add remaining oil. Drain cucumber. When oil just begins to smoke, add cucumber and stir-fry rapidly until heated through - about 30 seconds. Add pork and garlic; stir briefly. Add stock. Bring stock to boil; then stir in enough cornstarch paste to make a light gravy. Bring sauce to boil.
5. Serve.
